Name: UCSC Rocket Team (Rocket Slugs)
Relevance: Design, build, and launch high-powered rockets; competes annually in the NASA Student Launch competition.

Name: SlugSat
Relevance: Undergraduate-led CubeSat team selected by NASA's CubeSat Launch Initiative (CSLI) to deploy a satellite from the ISS.

Name: Santa Cruz Institute for Particle Physics (SCIPP)
Relevance: Conducts research in Atmospheric and Space Physics, including gamma-ray astronomy (Fermi telescope) and high-altitude balloon experiments.

Name: UCSC Planetary Science Group
Relevance: Studies solar system bodies using spacecraft data (Cassini, New Horizons, Europa Clipper) and numerical modeling of interiors and atmospheres.

Name: Astrobiology Initiative
Relevance: Interdisciplinary hub focused on the origins of life and planetary habitability, bridging EPS and Astronomy departments.

Name: Other Worlds Laboratory (OWL)
Relevance: Collaborative research group focused on exoplanets, solar system science, and planetary formation.

Name: Laboratory for Adaptive Optics (LAO)
Relevance: Develops advanced telescope technology to remove atmospheric blurring, supporting major projects like the Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT).

Name: University of California Observatories (UCO)
Relevance: Multi-campus research unit headquartered at UCSC that manages the Lick and Keck Observatories and designs space/ground instrumentation.

Name: GeoFly Lab
Relevance: Focuses on UAV-based mapping, GIS, and remote sensing for environmental monitoring and geospatial research.

Name: HARE Lab (Human Aware Robotics Exploration)
Relevance: Develops autonomous UAVs and ground robotics for field exploration, including lidar-based reconstruction and aerial surveying.

Name: Kudela Lab
Relevance: Utilizes satellite remote sensing imagery and robotic sensors to model ocean ecosystem dynamics and phytoplankton populations.

Name: Astronomy & Astrophysics Department
Relevance: UCSC's core academic unit for space science, heavily involved in JWST, DESI, and supercomputer-based astrophysical simulations.

Name: Thirty Meter Telescope (TMT) Project
Relevance: UCSC leads the technical development and UC participation in one of the world's largest next-generation ground-based telescopes.

Name: Plasma Analytical Lab (PAL)
Relevance: Uses plasma-based mass spectrometry to analyze geochemical samples, critical for studying planetary building blocks and meteorites.

Name: GIS & Drone Society
Relevance: Mentioned in 2025/2026 university events; likely a student club focused on UAV operations and spatial data.

Name: SOMARS (Slugbotics offshoot)
Relevance: New group focused on autonomous environmental survey drones; submitted proposals for CITRIS drone challenges.

Name: UCSC Astronomy Club
Relevance: Undergraduate organization for astronomy enthusiasts; active in community outreach and amateur observing.

Name: Formula Slug
Relevance: While primarily focused on electric vehicles, the team uses aerospace-adjacent composites and autonomous navigation technologies.
